creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

There was a build failure on amd64 as it was trying to install to
PREFIX/lib64 instead of PREFIX/lib. I've fixed this by adding
-DLIB_DIR=${PREFIX}/lib

You can see the updated patch here (includes some other minor cleanups
for recent ports standards):

http://people.freebsd.org/~bdrewery/patch-chinese-ibus-chewing.txt

However, there's still some issues with the build:

http://redports.org/~bdrewery/20120919004312-3401-68736/zh-ibus-chewing-1.4.2.log

The CMake scripts seem to be highly specific to Fedora.

1. It is trying to run 'date --utc', but this is not supported on
FreeBSD. Can you please check on this?

    -- Detecting CXX compiler ABI info - done
    *Hi
    date: illegal option -- -
    usage: date [-jnu] [-d dst] [-r seconds] [-t west]
[-v[+|-]val[ymwdHMS]] ...
                [-f fmt date | [[[[[cc]yy]mm]dd]HH]MM[.ss]] [+format]
    date: illegal option -- -
    usage: date [-jnu] [-d dst] [-r seconds] [-t west]
[-v[+|-]val[ymwdHMS]] ...
                [-f fmt date | [[[[[cc]yy]mm]dd]HH]MM[.ss]] [+format]

2. There are some warnings coming from generate-gconf-schemas. I'm not
sure if these are OK or not.

    (generate-gconf-schemas:57151): GLib-GObject-WARNING **: invalid
(NULL) pointer instance
    (generate-gconf-schemas:57151): GLib-GObject-CRITICAL **:
g_signal_connect_data: assertion `G_TYPE_CHECK_INSTANCE (instance)' failed


Regards,
Bryan Drewery
